priorities: Securing cutting‑edge treatment for daughter Chloe’s Parkinson’s disease
Long‑term wealth preservation and inter‑generational transmission
Maintaining large‑cap pharmaceutical and healthcare anchors as stable capital
Operational precision and steady growth of fixed‑income assets
Institutional stability and rigorous data‑driven execution
redLines: Divest immediately from any company that reduces or stops funding Parkinson’s or neuro‑degenerative research
Avoid investments that prioritize cheap lifestyle profits over core R&D commitments
Do not shift away from the core large‑cap pharma/healthcare holdings that support neuroscience
preferredSectors: Healthcare and biotech focused on neuro‑degenerative diseases
Blue‑chip large‑cap equities with strong R&D budgets
High‑quality fixed‑income for liquidity and cash‑flow matching
avoidedSectors: Companies abandoning or under‑funding Parkinson’s research
Lifestyle‑oriented consumer discretionary firms with low R&D spend

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term (multi‑generational wealth preservation with short‑term tactical allocations for health‑crisis funding)
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Moderate – needs cash for corporate liquidity, family expenses and rapid grant disbursements to the foundation

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe was studying abroad and doing well.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No immediate impact; reinforces family‑centric outlook.

lifeEvents.description: In‑depth discussion on long‑term wealth transmission strategies for the children.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced focus on stable, legacy‑preserving assets (large‑cap pharma, balanced allocation).

lifeEvents.description: Handover to new Relationship Manager Sarah Meier; client emphasized need for rigorous, data‑driven execution.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Continued adherence to existing Global Balanced Growth mandate.

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe diagnosed with rapidly progressing early‑onset Parkinson’s disease.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Shift of priority to funding neuro‑degenerative research; emotional re‑orientation of wealth purpose.

lifeEvents.description: Established a Venture Philanthropy foundation dedicated to neuro‑degenerative research.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Mandate realigned to ensure core holdings actively support brain‑disease research.

lifeEvents.description: Requested audit of European pharma holdings’ neuro‑degenerative clinical pipeline.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Increased monitoring of R&D commitments; potential divestments if pipelines are insufficient.

lifeEvents.description: Stated that any company abandoning Parkinson’s research would be a personal betrayal; demanded immediate flagging for divestment.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Explicit red‑line enforcement; active screening for R&D continuity.

lifeEvents.description: Transferred CHF 1 million from private portfolio to family foundation for an immediate grant to a neuro‑genetics lab in Geneva.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity re‑allocation toward targeted impact investments.

lifeEvents.description: Attended medical charity gala; reiterated need to keep capital flowing to leading neuroscience companies.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reaffirmed commitment to channel capital into research‑focused firms.

lifeEvents.description: Requested expedited statement for foundation’s clinical audit while visiting a Boston movement‑disorder research hub.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Continued focus on transparency and rapid funding of promising research.

lifeEvents.description: Met a biotech firm with a breakthrough Parkinson’s asset; emphasized large‑cap holdings as primary financial support while heart lies with neuroscience breakthroughs.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential tilt toward strategic biotech exposure aligned with foundation goals.

personalProfile.interests: automotive engineering, large‑cap healthcare, neuroscience research, art sponsorship
personalProfile.quirks: deeply emotional when discussing his daughter's health, prefers data‑driven analysis
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of cutting‑edge neurodegenerative research, Swiss precision engineering, family legacy
personalProfile.avoidTopics: competitor banks, political debates, sports rivalries
personalProfile.kidsEducation: daughter Chloe studying abroad, now focused on health and potential specialized education

keyQuotes: My automotive business means nothing if I cannot save my daughter. Everything has changed.
Our private wealth is now a weapon to save Chloe. I want our core healthcare and pharma holdings to actively support the entities pushing the boundaries of brain disease research.
If a company we own ever abandons or defunds Parkinson's research to chase cheap lifestyle profits, it would feel like a personal betrayal of my daughter. Flag them immediately for divestment.
Seeing these brilliant researchers is our only hope. We must keep our capital flowing to the companies leading the fight against this disease.
We want our large‑cap holdings to act as our primary financial support system, while our hearts belong to neuroscience breakthroughs.
